rice.name: Brown Planthopper (BPH)
rice.symptoms: Plants turn yellow and dry in patches. Small brown insects at base of plants. 'Hopper burn' — circular dead patches.
treatment: Remove excess nitrogen fertilization
Drain field for 3-4 days
Spray Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100ml/ha or Buprofezin 25 SC @ 500ml/ha
Encourage natural predators: spiders, mirid bugs
Avoid synchronous planting in large areas

rice.name: Blast (Magnaporthe oryzae)
rice.symptoms: Diamond-shaped grey lesions on leaves with dark borders. Neck blast causes white, broken panicles.
treatment: Use resistant varieties (check local recommendations)
Avoid excess nitrogen
Spray Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 0.6g/L or Isoprothiolane 40 EC @ 1.5ml/L
Maintain balanced nutrition — excess N makes it worse
Destroy crop residue after harvest

rice.name: Sheath Blight
rice.symptoms: Oval or irregular greenish-grey lesions on leaf sheath, near water line. Lesions enlarge and merge.
treatment: Wider spacing for air circulation
Avoid deep water stagnation
Spray Validamycin 3% L @ 2ml/L or Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 2ml/L
Reduce plant density
Balanced fertilizer — avoid excess N

wheat.name: Rust (Yellow, Brown, Black)
wheat.symptoms: Yellow/brown/black powdery pustules on leaves in rows. Yellow rust most common in North India.
treatment: Grow resistant varieties (check current recommended list)
Spray Propiconazole 25 EC @ 1ml/L at first sign
Spray Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 1ml/L as alternative
Early sowing helps avoid late rust
Remove volunteer wheat plants

wheat.name: Aphids
wheat.symptoms: Small green/black insects on ear heads and upper leaves. Sticky honeydew. Sooty mold develops.
treatment: Spray Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1ml/L
Spray at milky stage if >5 aphids per ear head
Encourage ladybird beetles (natural predator)
Avoid excessive irrigation in late season

cotton.name: Bollworm (American, Pink)
cotton.symptoms: Small entry holes in squares/bolls. Caterpillars inside bolls. Damaged flowers and bolls drop.
treatment: Install pheromone traps (5/ha) for monitoring
Spray Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 0.4ml/L when threshold crossed
Hand-pick and destroy affected bolls
Avoid late-season irrigation that prolongs crop
Deep summer plowing to destroy pupae

cotton.name: Whitefly
cotton.symptoms: Small white flies under leaves. Yellowing and leaf curling. Sooty mold on honeydew.
treatment: Spray Thiamethoxam 25 WG @ 0.5g/L or Acetamiprid 20 SP @ 0.5g/L
Install yellow sticky traps (12/ha)
Avoid continuous cotton — rotate with non-host crops
Spray neem oil (1500 ppm) @ 3ml/L as organic option

tomato.name: Early Blight
tomato.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings (target/bullseye pattern) on older leaves. Leaves turn yellow and drop.
treatment: Remove and destroy affected lower leaves
Spray Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5g/L or Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 2g/L
Mulch around plants to prevent soil splash
Maintain adequate spacing for air circulation
Crop rotation — avoid planting after potatoes/tomatoes

tomato.name: Late Blight
tomato.symptoms: Water-soaked dark green to brown spots. White fuzzy growth on undersides in humid conditions. Rapid plant death.
treatment: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil MZ) @ 2.5g/L preventively
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Avoid overhead irrigation
Ensure good drainage
Plant resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak, etc.)

tomato.name: Fruit Borer (Helicoverpa)
tomato.symptoms: Small holes in fruits with caterpillar inside. Frass visible near entry hole. Damaged fruits rot.
treatment: Install pheromone traps (5/ha) for monitoring
Spray Emamectin benzoate 5 SG @ 0.4g/L
Release Trichogramma egg parasitoid @ 50,000/ha
Hand-pick and destroy caterpillars in small plots
Intercrop with marigold as trap crop

potato.name: Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans)
potato.symptoms: Water-soaked dark brown patches on leaves. White mold on underside. Tuber rot with reddish-brown dry rot.
treatment: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil MZ) @ 2.5g/L
Spray Cymoxanil + Mancozeb as alternative
Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Surya)
Avoid waterlogging — ensure good drainage
Destroy infected tubers — do not store
